item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd point out that they've got enough anger and freedom to flood their summoner's houses- these are just the reasonable delegates chosen to speak for the union. (And also -D was with them.) Makes sense that they'd be fairly reasonable and well mannered- the Foundation's just playing the neutral mediator here trying to minimize the amount of chaos that is generated; they probably wouldn't be involved at all if it weren't for the collateral damage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it as is, but I think it probably would be more interesting if the negotiations had some more weight. By definition these are manual laborers being manipulated by educated people that give them little consideration, with little ability to resist the demands placed on them (I'd hate to be a nymph summoned by those sleezes…). A bit more anger and a little less "concerned citizens coalition" would probably be appropriate.

If this was fleshed out more, we could end up seeing the Foundation functioning like the Pinkertons, at least initially. Once they figured out the summoners were abusing the protection being provided they might change direction, of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm in two minds about this one. On the one hand, it's fun, the log in particular is well written and easy to read. I can't fault the presentation of it.

On the other, I feel like the concept of summoned elementals engaging in an industrial dispute with the summoner is interesting enough that I'd liked to have seen it elaborated on in a piece with a more serious element. As it is, I read the Containment Procedures and Description and expected a tense piece of diplomacy or something nuanced with the Foundation inadvertently caught between two entities, trying to pursue an agenda that was at odds with both the parties involved in the dispute. While I can't fault the effectiveness of the resulting humour, it feels a waste to spend such a promising concept on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, there's always one coffee abuser in the house. Great job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Water Wizard Worker Association.
Lets be honest here: if you hadn't done it, Oglaf would've.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was amused, but the interviews dipped into a bit too much silliness for me to take seriously. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's always nice to see people enjoy the silly stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this one. It was just fun and light, and sometimes we all need that, on this the Foundation. I don't care if it's weird tone or not. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We need more stakeholder participation in thaumaturgy!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I, for one, support direct action in the servitorian's struggle against the exploitative summoning class.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hah! Now that's not a comparison I expected to hear. Oddly apt though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this is good. Reminds me of Oglaf.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked it. The conversations of the agents felt a little of tone, but, man, they were priceless.

+1. Hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for "You have a family, you deviant!" Also water demon union. Though I do think it's a little explainy in the description, c.f. "Said abilities are likely the reason the entities comprising SCP-2185 originally began to be employed by the aforementioned individuals."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Delicious. I wonder if the writer frequents /tg/.

Wizards, no sense of right and wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me at an Elemental Labor Union. The rest was delicious gravy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep. Thanks for the correction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Unrelated populous" - should that be "unrelated populace"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, that was my singular contribution to the log, actually. Glad you enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, no response in particular until I got about halfway through the negotiations, and the bit about the coffee break.

Now I have to give you a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unionized water wizards using the Foundation as contact negotiators is fantastic.

Also of note, I made a minor grammar fix. Feel free to revert if I'm just an idiot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The logs were rather fun to write - they really basically wrote themselves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this a lot mainly because I know what it's like to sit through arbitration meetings. This was cheeky and neat. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A collaboration between me and Djoric, who did most of the work on the log.

A fun, simple SCP, I think.
